name: History-Resolved Loop-Coherent Chern Self-Healing Law
description: Lineage-preserving synthesis of the history-resolved adaptive-ruler phase update, the plaquette-coherence order parameter, and the adaptive Chern self-healing conductance law. It promotes loop coherence from a passive observable to a bounded gain gate on the existing self-healing dynamics, so reinforcement is strongest when current flow, branch-resolved phase memory, and plaquette holonomy agree. The law unifies branch memory, loop locking, and local Chern feedback in one update, recovers the parent self-healing law when kappa_Psi=0, and recovers a loop-aware phase-memory ARP/EGATL law when chi=0 and C_loc=0.
source: derived: History-Resolved Phase with Adaptive Ruler + Topological Coherence Order Parameter + Adaptive Chern Self-Healing Conductance Law
assumptions: The ARP / adaptive-pi_a framework and units from the parent TopEquations lineage are inherited without changing the base conductance state variables.
Plaquette structure and orientation signs sigma_{p,e} are well-defined on a fixed lattice so Theta_p is computed consistently over the monitored loop family.
Psi is evaluated over a fixed plaquette set with slowly varying topology and pi_a > 0 throughout the run.
kappa_Psi >= 0 is chosen so the loop-coherence gate 1 + kappa_Psi Psi remains bounded and nonnegative on the regimes of interest.
chi >= 0 and the inherited rates alpha_G(S), mu_G(S), and lambda_s retain the same meaning and units as in the parent history-resolved self-healing law.
evidence: Builds directly on History-Resolved Phase with Adaptive Ruler, Topological Coherence Order Parameter (ARP Locking), and Adaptive Chern Self-Healing Conductance Law: Eq. (1) is the parent branch-safe state update, Eq. (2) is the plaquette-coherence observable, and Eq. (3) inserts that observable as a bounded gate on the inherited self-healing conductance law.
Recovers the existing Chern self-healing conductance law exactly when kappa_Psi = 0.
Recovers a loop-aware phase-memory ARP/EGATL conductance law when chi = 0 and C_loc = 0, while preserving the adaptive-ruler suppression backbone.

Dimension check: Psi, kappa_Psi, theta_{R,e}/pi_a, and C_loc are dimensionless, so alpha_G(S)(1+kappa_Psi Psi)|J_e|, mu_G(S)g_e, lambda_s g_e sin^2(theta_{R,e}/2pi_a), and chi C_loc g_e all retain conductance-per-time units.

Executable validation target: on matched damage/recovery runs, higher-Psi trajectories should correlate with stronger boundary fraction, transfer efficiency, and post-damage healing stability at fixed chi and comparable injury protocol.
